text.title: FinOps Implementation Checklist
text.summary: Ordered checklist for implementing FinOps Inform, Optimize, and Operate phases.

rule.statement: Enable detailed billing exports with daily refresh before starting any optimization work.
rule.rationale: Optimization decisions made without current data cause savings estimates to be wrong and commitments to be over-purchased.

list.item: Enable AWS CUR / GCP BigQuery export / Azure Cost Management export
Define mandatory tag schema: Environment, Owner, Project, CostCenter
Implement tag enforcement policies (SCP/Policy) to block untagged launches
Create untagged resource report and assign remediation owners
Enable cloud-native anomaly detection with Slack/email routing
Build team-level cost dashboards with daily/weekly reports
Target >80% tag coverage initially; >90% at mature stage
Run weekly rightsizing analysis: flag instances with avg CPU <20% or memory <30%
Identify idle instances (>7 days <5% CPU): schedule termination or stop
Implement auto-shutdown tags for non-production resources
Analyze 30+ days of usage before purchasing Savings Plans or Reserved Instances
Delete orphaned snapshots, unattached EBS volumes, unused Elastic IPs
Implement S3 lifecycle policies: Standard-IA at 30d, Glacier at 90d, Deep Archive at 365d
For AI/ML training: migrate on-demand GPU to Spot with checkpointing
For AI/ML inference: implement caching and batching before scaling GPU instances

rule.statement: Establish per-team budgets with 50/80/100/120% alerts and a forecasted-overage alert. Review forecast accuracy monthly.
rule.rationale: Teams that never see their costs have no incentive to optimize. Multi-threshold alerts create early warning before budgets are blown.

list.item: Set monthly/quarterly budgets per team and environment
Configure budget alerts at 50%, 80%, 100%, 120% thresholds
Add forecasted-overage alert at 100% (fires before month end)
Conduct monthly cost reviews with action items and savings tracking
Track cost per unit of work (cost per 100k tokens, cost per transaction)
Automate tagging remediation for new untagged resources
Crawl: basic visibility, initial tagging, manual reviews, reactive
Walk: >80% tags, regular reviews, some automation, proactive rightsizing
Run: >90% tags, automated optimization, real-time visibility, cost-aware culture
